title: Abutilon abutilon
taxonomy: Abutilon abutilon is a member of the Malvaceae family and is classified as a genus within the family. The Malvaceae family is a diverse group of flowering plants that includes hibiscus, hollyhock, and okra. Abutilon abutilon is native to the Americas and is a type of flowering shrub. The plant is commonly known as the Chinese bellflower or flowering maple, due to its maple-like leaves and bell-shaped flowers. It is often grown as an ornamental plant in warm and temperate climates.
uses: Abutilon abutilon, also known as the muslin mallow, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. intro: Abutilon abutilon, also known as Indian mallow or velvet leaf, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and digestive issues.
